title: Estimate multi-digit multiplication
description: Use estimation to find a reasonable solution to multi-digit multiplication problems.
topic_path: Module 2: Multi-digit whole number and decimal fraction operations
Topic A: Mental strategies for multi-digit whole number multiplication

hints:
Let's estimate the product by rounding the factors:

$\phantom{=} 29 \times 3{,}198 $

$\approx 30 \times 3{,}000$

Let's estimate the product by rounding the factors:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} 84 \times 6{,}879\\\\
&\approx 80 \times 7{,}000
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} \blueD{80} \times \greenD{7{,}000}\\\\
&= (\blueD{8} \times \blueD{10})  \times (\greenD{7} \times \greenD{1{,}000})&\small\gray{\text{Factor out multiples of 10}}\\\\
&= (\blueD{8} \times \greenD{7})  \times (\blueD{10} \times \greenD{1{,}000})&\small\gray{\text{Group the multiples of 10}}\\\\
&= 56  \times 10{,}000
\end{align}$

Let's estimate the product by rounding the factors:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} 48 \times 366\\\\
&\approx 50 \times 400
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} \blueD{50} \times \greenD{400}\\\\
&= (\blueD{5} \times \blueD{10})  \times (\greenD{4} \times \greenD{100})&\small\gray{\text{Factor out multiples of 10}}\\\\
&= (\blueD{5} \times \greenD{4})  \times (\blueD{10} \times \greenD{100})&\small\gray{\text{Group the multiples of 10}}\\\\
&= 20\times 1{,}000
\end{align}$
